questions.prompt: An agent's `issue_refund` call timed out. The agent retried. Two refunds went out. What's the right design fix?

choices.text: Tell the agent not to retry.

Require an `idempotency_key` on side-effect tools. Generate the key once per task; pass it on every retry. Your handler stores `(key → result)` for a few days; repeat calls return the stored result instead of doing the work twice. Stripe-style idempotency, applied to agent tools.

questions.explanation: Idempotency keys are the standard fix for safe retries on side-effect operations. The agent generates a UUID once; your handler dedups based on it. Double-refunds, double-emails, double-charges all become impossible — even when the network drops mid-call.

questions.prompt: What should a tool return on failure?

choices.text: A raw exception or stack trace.

A structured error: `{ error: 'NOT_FOUND', message: 'Order O-... not found.', suggested_action: 'Try search_orders.' }`. With `is_error: true` set so the agent knows it's a failure. Standardized error codes across tools so the agent learns the patterns.

questions.explanation: Structured, actionable errors let the agent recover. Stack traces are noise. Standardized error codes (NOT_FOUND, UNAUTHORIZED, RATE_LIMIT, VALIDATION, CONFLICT, UPSTREAM_ERROR, TIMEOUT) across tools form a vocabulary the agent learns — it knows what to retry, what to escalate, what to tell the user.
